serverFastDl.title: FastDL
serverFastDl.description: Configure Fast Download (FastDL) to serve files directly from your Wings node via nginx for faster download speeds.

serverFastDl.infoDescription: FastDL allows game servers to serve files directly from the Wings node via nginx, providing faster download speeds for players. This is particularly useful for Source Engine games (CS:GO, TF2, Garry's Mod, etc.) and other games that support custom download URLs. After enabling FastDL, configure your game server to use the provided URL.
serverFastDl.step1: Upload your game files to /fastdl in your server's file manager
serverFastDl.step2: Copy the command above and add it to your server configuration
serverFastDl.step3: Players will download maps/mods from your FastDL server instead of your game server
serverFastDl.step4: This reduces server load and speeds up downloads for players

what.title: What are VM Plans?
what.description: VM Plans define the hardware specifications (CPU, RAM, disk, network) that will be applied when provisioning a virtual machine or container on a Proxmox node.

qemu.title: QEMU/KVM Virtual Machines
qemu.description: Full hardware virtualisation — runs any OS. Supports CPU passthrough, live migration, snapshots, and nested virtualisation.

lxc.title: LXC Containers
lxc.description: OS-level containers sharing the host kernel. Lower overhead than full VMs, faster boot times, and more efficient for Linux workloads.

templates.title: What are VM Templates?
templates.description: Templates are pre-built VM images (e.g. Ubuntu 22.04, Debian 12) that Proxmox clones when creating a new VM. For QEMU/KVM plans, creating an instance always uses a template — the panel clones that VM and applies the plan's CPU, RAM and disk. LXC can use different flows.
templates.step1: On your Proxmox host: create a VM, install the OS, clean it (e.g. remove machine-id, cloud-init if needed), then in the Proxmox UI right‑click the VM → Convert to template.
templates.step2: In FeatherPanel: go to VM Nodes (VDS Nodes), open the node that runs that Proxmox host, and open the Templates tab. Add a template: give it a name and the Template VMID (the VMID of the template VM in Proxmox). Once registered, that template appears when creating a VM instance from a plan on this node.

You can also bring your own spells – import them here or host them in your own repo.
